note: Verified-open Santa Cruz County gardening resources. Source of truth: Local Resources/Santa_Cruz_County_Resource_Master_List.md. Unconfirmed entries are intentionally excluded until verified.

categories.label: Nurseries & Garden Centers
categories.blurb: Where to buy plants, starts, and trees from people who know our microclimates.

items.name: San Lorenzo Garden Center
items.blurb: Full-service garden center, 40+ years; plants, tools, and supplies.

items.name: DIG Gardens
items.blurb: Succulents, drought-hardy and architectural plants, pottery, and workshops.

items.name: DIG Gardens (Aptos)
items.blurb: Second DIG location: houseplants, pots, and garden gifts.

items.name: The Garden Company
items.blurb: Family-owned since 1986; drought-tolerant, organic veg and herb starts.

items.name: Far West Nursery
items.blurb: Quality plants, California natives, and organic soils; open daily.

items.name: Scarborough Gardens
items.blurb: Nursery plus patio and gift shop.

items.name: Sierra Azul Nursery & Gardens
items.blurb: California native and Mediterranean-climate plants; demonstration garden.

items.name: Native Revival Nursery
items.blurb: California natives and drought-tolerant plants; landscape design help.

items.name: Central Coast Wilds
items.blurb: Watershed-specific California natives. Walk-ins Mon-Fri 10-3.

items.name: Mountain Feed & Farm Supply
items.blurb: Mountain garden nursery plus seeds, soils, feed, and homesteading supply.

categories.label: Bulk Soil & Compost
categories.blurb: Buy soil, compost, and mulch by the cubic yard. Far cheaper than bags for raised beds.

items.name: Central Home Supply
items.blurb: OMRI organic compost, topsoil, mushroom compost, and mulch; delivery available.

items.name: Aptos Landscape Supply
items.blurb: Bulk soils and One Earth Compost; locally owned since 1966.

items.name: Evergreen Supply
items.blurb: Bulk soil mixes, OMRI compost, and custom blends; established 1961.

items.name: Buena Vista Landfill & Recycling Center (Santa Cruz County)
items.blurb: County sells compost, soil amendment, and mulch to the public; delivery for large loads.

categories.label: Seeds & Seed Libraries
categories.blurb: Local seed companies and free seed libraries suited to coastal and microclimate growing.

items.name: Renee's Garden Seeds
items.blurb: Felton-based seed company. Buy online or at local nurseries that carry the line.

items.name: Felton Seed Lending Library (Santa Cruz Public Libraries)
items.blurb: Free seeds, no library card needed. Borrow, plant, and donate back.

items.name: Mountain Feed & Farm Supply
items.blurb: Organic and heirloom vegetable and herb seeds, starts, and seed mixes.

categories.label: Learn & Get Advice
categories.blurb: Free, research-based gardening help and classes from local experts.

items.name: UC Master Gardeners (Monterey, San Benito & Santa Cruz)
items.blurb: Free research-based gardening advice, classes, and plant clinics.

items.name: UCSC Center for Agroecology (Farm & Garden)
items.blurb: Public workshops, Friends of the Farm & Garden events, and the annual spring plant sale.

items.name: Cabrillo College Horticulture
items.blurb: Classes, greenhouses, and student-grown plant sales.

items.name: CA Native Plant Society, Santa Cruz Chapter
items.blurb: Native-plant talks, restoration teams, and spring and fall native plant sales.

items.name: UC Cooperative Extension, Santa Cruz County
items.blurb: County extension office; hosts the Master Gardener program.

items.name: The Gardeners' Club of Santa Cruz County
items.blurb: Monthly meetings, speakers, and member garden resources.

categories.label: Farmers Markets
categories.blurb: Fresh local produce, starts, and seedlings. Days and locations across the county.

items.name: Downtown Santa Cruz Farmers Market
items.blurb: Wednesdays 12:30-5pm, year-round.

items.name: Westside Farmers Market
items.blurb: Saturdays 9am-1pm, year-round.

items.name: Live Oak / Eastside Farmers Market
items.blurb: Sundays 9am-1pm, year-round.

items.name: Felton Farmers Market
items.blurb: Tuesday afternoons, seasonal (reopens in spring).

items.name: Scotts Valley Farmers Market
items.blurb: Saturdays 9am-1pm, seasonal (reopens in spring).

items.name: Watsonville Farmers Market
items.blurb: Fridays 2-7pm, year-round.

items.name: Aptos Farmers Market at Cabrillo College
items.blurb: Saturdays 8am-12pm, year-round; around 90 vendors.

categories.label: Community Gardens & Nonprofits
categories.blurb: Garden plots, farm projects, and volunteer opportunities close to home.

items.name: Homeless Garden Project
items.blurb: Organic farm with job training, a CSA, U-pick, and a farm stand.

items.name: Scotts Valley Community Garden
items.blurb: Resident garden plots, assigned yearly starting in February.

items.name: Watsonville Community Gardens
items.blurb: Low-cost garden boxes with neighborhood-resident priority.

items.name: Beach Flats Community Garden
items.blurb: Neighborhood organic garden with plots for local residents.

categories.label: Feed & Farm Supply
categories.blurb: Chicken feed, seasonal chicks, and homestead supplies for backyard flocks.

items.name: Scotts Valley Feed
items.blurb: Feed and barn supplies; seasonal baby chicks (March-August).

items.name: Westside Farm and Feed
items.blurb: Family-run; chicken and livestock feed, hay, bedding, and coops.

items.name: Corralitos Feed & Pet Supplies
items.blurb: Poultry and livestock feed, tack, and supplies for backyard flocks.

items.name: Mountain Feed & Farm Supply
items.blurb: Feed, poultry supplies, incubators, and beekeeping gear.

items.name: Tractor Supply Co.
items.blurb: Coops, feeders, and waterers; seasonal Chick Days live chicks.

categories.label: Hardware & Garden
categories.blurb: Independent hardware stores for tools, drip irrigation, and garden supplies.

items.name: Santa Cruz Ace Hardware
items.blurb: Independent Ace; hardware, garden supplies, and tools.

items.name: Aptos Ace Hardware
items.blurb: Independent Ace with a garden section.
